summary_one_line: SQL text whose parse yields an OP_ShiftRight/OP_ShiftLeft VDBE opcode, i.e. a '>>' or '<<' bit-shift operator inside a parseable SELECT expression.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes that the SQL tokenizer accepts as a valid expression containing a bit-shift operator ('>>' = 0x3e 0x3e, or '<<'), so the code generator emits an OP_ShiftRight/OP_ShiftLeft opcode that the VDBE then dispatches via the shared 'case OP_ShiftRight' at sqlite3.c:86941. Reaching it requires a SELECT-like prefix plus a two-operand shift sub-expression.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed SELECT statements carrying shift/comparison operator bytes: Seed 1 'SELECT 1*9988878...>9=...' and Seed 3 'sELECT' followed by repeated '>>' (0x3e 0x3e) runs. The BYTE DIFF shows the winner concentrates the readable prefix S/E/L/E/C/T and '>'(0x3e) bytes at the operand offsets, and OP_ShiftRight is hit T=6 on the winner side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds are short, syntactically broken byte soup dominated by ';'(0x3b), '+'(0x2b) and high bytes (e.g. Seed 1 'i;;;;;;; . Z;ELE', Seed 2 '#1;.. S..#$'); they never form a parseable shift expression, so the parser/codegen never emits the opcode and OP_ShiftRight stays T=0 (blocked) for the loser.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted CMPs (the tokenizer/keyword matching and operator-character comparisons) get progressively closer to matching valid SQL keyword/operator literals; the distance-bucket signal preserves partial-prefix SELECT/shift-operator inputs that naive's plain edge feedback cannot ratchet toward, letting value_profile assemble a parseable '>>' expression.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the shared OP_ShiftRight/OP_ShiftLeft case in the VDBE main dispatch loop, requiring a shift opcode to be emitted.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Source line: case OP_ShiftRight: {           /* same as TK_RSHIFT, in1, in2, out3 */

evidence_trail.claim: OP_ShiftLeft falls through into the OP_ShiftRight case, so either '>>' or '<<' reaches the blocker.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: case OP_ShiftLeft:              /* same as TK_LSHIFT, in1, in2, out3 */

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks it in 9 of 10.
A winner-resolving seed is a SELECT with a multiply/comparison expression carrying '>' bytes.
A winner-resolving seed contains literal '>>' (0x3e 0x3e) right-shift operator bytes.
Loser-blocking naive seeds are short, non-parseable byte soup dominated by ';' and high bytes, not valid SQL.
OP_ShiftRight is taken (true) 6 times on the winner side but never on the loser side, confirming the directional divergence.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L86941  T=6 F=348  T=0 F=40  case OP_ShiftRight: {           /* same as TK_RSHIFT, in1...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner concentrates readable SELECT-prefix bytes at the leading offsets where the loser shows scattered ';'/high bytes.
value_profile adds a corpus entry whenever an input's CMP operands get closer to matching than any seen before, providing the gradient naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The CMP_MAP distance bucket encodes matching-prefix length for string/memory comparisons, the signal that ratchets toward valid keyword/operator literals.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is not required for CMP_MAP gradient (only I2SRandReplace mandates a db_query.py lineage check). The winner is value_profile, whose MECHANISM CONTEXT explicitly states it uses 'No I2SRandReplace' and logs 'havoc/token names only', so no dash-row I2S floor is expected; the gradient signal is the distance-bucketed CMP_MAP feedback, evidenced by the source/byte trail rather than lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the SQL operator-character CMPs are made non-intercepted (e.g. a single-byte equality not exposed to CMP_MAP) would predict value_profile loses its edge over naive; if value_profile still resolves OP_ShiftRight at the same rate with no operator-prefix CMP gradient available, the CMP_MAP-gradient attribution is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether value_profile's win is specifically driven by operator-literal CMP gradient versus simply preserving more SELECT-prefix partial inputs via tokenizer keyword CMPs is not directly distinguishable from the seed bytes alone.
